updates.title: Claude Fable 5 & Mythos 5 Launch + Multi-Platform API Updates
updates.summary: Anthropic officially released Claude Fable 5 (claude-fable-5) on June 9, 2026 — the first Mythos-class model made publicly available — alongside the invitation-only Claude Mythos 5 for Project Glasswing partners. Claude Fable 5 is available across Claude API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, Microsoft Foundry, and Claude.ai, and is included free for Pro, Max, Team, and seat-based Enterprise plans through June 22 before requiring usage credits. The release is accompanied by major platform changes: Agent SDK and claude -p programmatic usage will draw from a separate $20/month credit pool starting June 15; Claude Code v2.1.169 (June 8) adds safe mode and a /cd command; Claude is now available on Apple devices (iOS/macOS/iPadOS/visionOS/watchOS 27); and Claude Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 models retire from the API on June 15, 2026.
updates.accuracy_notes: Official release notes from Anthropic's own docs portal — highest reliability. Launch date of June 9, 2026 confirmed across TechCrunch, CNBC, VentureBeat, and AWS/Azure/GCP partner blogs. Free-access window 'through June 22' confirmed by multiple secondary sources. The $20 Agent SDK credit pool change is confirmed. Apple device support is consistent with iOS 27/macOS 27 timeline. Deprecation dates for Sonnet 4/Opus 4 on June 15 are documented in model-deprecations page.

updates.title: Anthropic just publically released its Mythos-grade AI at no extra cost for a limited time
updates.summary: XDA Developers covers Anthropic's launch of Claude Fable 5, framing it as a landmark moment where Mythos-grade intelligence — previously locked away from the public — is now accessible to regular subscribers at no extra charge for a limited window. The article highlights the time-limited free access through June 23 for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plan holders, explains that safeguards kick in for roughly less than 5% of sessions (routing to Opus 4.8 in sensitive topic areas like cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, and distillation), and contextualizes Fable 5 as the same underlying model weights as Mythos 5 but with an extra product-layer of safety controls. The piece underscores that this is the first time Anthropic has made a Mythos-class model available outside of its tightly controlled Project Glasswing program.
updates.accuracy_notes: XDA is reporting on the official Anthropic announcement. The 'less than 5% of sessions' safeguard figure and the June 22/23 free access window are consistent across all sources. Minor discrepancy: XDA says 'June 23' while Anthropic's own release notes say 'through June 22' — likely a time-zone rounding difference, not a factual error. The Mythos 5 model weights claim is confirmed in Anthropic's official system card. Overall reliability: high.

updates.title: Claude in your browser is a security risk, and this year proved it
updates.summary: XDA Developers published a retrospective security analysis on June 10-11, 2026, reviewing two significant vulnerabilities discovered in Claude's Chrome extension in 2026. The first, ShadowPrompt (disclosed March 2026 by Koi Security researcher Oren Yomtov), allowed any malicious website to inject instructions directly into Claude in Chrome via prompt injection. The second, ClaudeBleed (disclosed publicly May 2026 by LayerX, after private disclosure to Anthropic in late April), allowed a completely separate malicious Chrome extension with zero declared permissions to send commands directly to the Claude extension and execute them. Anthropic shipped a partial fix in version 1.0.70, but LayerX states the root cause has still not been fully resolved, leaving users at ongoing risk when using Claude in browser contexts.
updates.accuracy_notes: XDA is synthesizing confirmed disclosures from Koi Security (ShadowPrompt) and LayerX (ClaudeBleed). Both vulnerability disclosures are corroborated by The Hacker News, eWeek, and the original research blogs. The 'version 1.0.70 partial fix' claim and 'root cause unresolved' assertion come from LayerX's disclosure — should be fact-checked against Anthropic's official security advisory if one exists. Note: the underlying security incidents occurred in March-May 2026; the XDA article is a recap/analysis piece published June 10-11. The article is primarily editorial in nature, not a new security disclosure.
